trainsOnDataNuance: Free, Plus, and Pro accounts train on your data by default. API, Team, and Enterprise accounts are excluded from training.
optOutHow: Go to Settings > Data Controls and toggle off 'Improve the model for everyone'. If you do not have an account, you can submit a request through the OpenAI Privacy Center.
retentionPeriod: Chat history stays indefinitely if enabled. If disabled, data is kept for 30 days for abuse monitoring, then deleted.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: You can delete individual chats or your entire account. Deletion takes up to 30 days. Backup copies may remain for a short time after.
thirdPartySharing: Shared with vendors, service providers, and affiliates for operations, security monitoring, and legal compliance. Not sold to advertisers.
humanReviewConditions: A sample of chats may be reviewed by human trainers to improve safety and performance. This does not apply if you have opted out of training or use enterprise/API services.
regionalVariation: EEA, UK, and Swiss users follow a separate privacy policy with full GDPR rights, including the right to object and request deletion through dedicated regional compliance workflows.
childrenDataPolicy: Minimum age is 13 (or regional equivalent). Users under 18 need parental or guardian consent. OpenAI does not knowingly collect data from children under 13.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Enterprise (Team and Enterprise tiers) and API accounts do not train on your data by default. They also offer custom retention agreements and compliance certifications such as SOC 2 and GDPR.

trainsOnDataNuance: Consumer chats on Claude Free and Pro require user opt-in for model training. Console API and Enterprise plans are excluded from training.
optOutHow: Go to Claude Settings > Data Privacy Controls and toggle off 'Help improve Claude'. You can also request opt-out through the Privacy Center.
retentionPeriod: If training is disabled, data is kept for 30 days. If enabled, de-identified data may be retained up to 5 years for research and model development.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: You can delete chats from the active UI. Account deletion requests are typically completed within 30 days.
thirdPartySharing: Shared with hosting providers (AWS, Google Cloud) and service providers under strict data privacy agreements. No sharing with advertisers.
humanReviewConditions: Safety reviews happen when conversations are flagged for abuse or terms of service violations. Human reviews are minimized for opted-out users.
regionalVariation: GDPR baseline applies for EU/UK users. Anthropic has designated EU and UK representatives for data protection inquiries.
childrenDataPolicy: Requires users to be at least 13 years old. Users under 18 need permission from a parent or guardian.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Console API and Claude Enterprise accounts do not train on your data by default. They offer custom retention options and HIPAA/SOC 2 compliance.

trainsOnDataNuance: Consumer Gemini apps train on chats by default when Gemini Apps Activity is on. Audio and Gemini Live recordings are not used for training. Workspace and Cloud (Vertex AI) do not train on user data.
optOutHow: Turn off Gemini Apps Activity at myactivity.google.com. Future chats will not be used for training. You can also delete past chats or use Temporary Chats. EU/UK users have GDPR right to object.
retentionPeriod: Default 18 months, adjustable to 3 or 36 months. Temporary chats and Keep Activity off: 72 hours. Reviewed chats: up to 3 years, disconnected from account. Audio recordings: deleted after 3 years of inactivity.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Delete individual chats or clear all Gemini Apps Activity anytime. Chats already reviewed by humans are retained up to 3 years disconnected from your account. Turning off Keep Activity does not delete data in other Google services.
thirdPartySharing: Shared with service providers for human review (disconnected from account first). Connected third-party apps receive data per their own privacy policies. Google does not sell personal data.
humanReviewConditions: A subset of chats is reviewed by human annotators to improve models, reduce unintended activations, and keep Gemini safe. Reviewers assess response quality, accuracy, and safety. Turning off Keep Activity stops future chats from being reviewed for improvement.
regionalVariation: EU/UK users have GDPR rights including right to object to processing and request data correction. Korea-based users are subject to Korean Location Terms. Some regions have different Activity defaults or require explicit consent.
childrenDataPolicy: Available to users 13+ (or regional age of consent). Teens have specific default settings. Google applies strict safety filters for teen interactions.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Workspace, Vertex AI, and Cloud customers are covered under enterprise agreements with no data training, no human review, and local residency support. Separate Generative AI in Google Workspace Privacy Hub applies.

trainsOnDataNuance: Meta trains on public content (posts, comments, photos, audio) shared on Facebook and Instagram by default. Interactions with Meta AI features are also used for training. Information from partners, vendors, and third parties is also used.
optOutHow: EU/UK users can use the Right to Object form in the Privacy Center under GDPR. US and other non-EU users can set accounts to private to restrict training data, but there is no general opt-out setting in the privacy policy.
retentionPeriod: Retained on a case-by-case basis. Meta considers whether data is needed to operate products, the feature and how it works, legal obligations, and other legitimate purposes such as preventing harm or investigating violations.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Users can delete information or their account through provided tools. However, public posts already used in model training cannot be recalled.
thirdPartySharing: Shared across Meta platforms and with advertisers, analytics partners, integrated partners, measurement vendors, marketing vendors, service providers, external researchers, and AI integrations. Meta does not sell information to anyone.
humanReviewConditions: Manual review conducted for safety, integrity, and security purposes: verifying accounts, finding policy violations, investigating suspicious activity, detecting and preventing harmful or unlawful behavior. Some decisions are reviewed by Oversight Board.
regionalVariation: EU and UK users have GDPR rights including right to object to AI training. US users can reference the United States Regional Privacy Notice. Data Protection Authorities (DPAs) are available in other jurisdictions.
childrenDataPolicy: Services are for users 13+. Meta does not knowingly target AI products directly at children under 13. Public posts from teen users may be collected if publicly shared.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Meta does not offer a hosted enterprise AI service with dedicated B2B privacy guarantees. Llama models run on third-party enterprise platforms under those platforms' terms.

trainsOnDataNuance: Consumer Copilot (free and Pro) uses conversations for training by default. Enterprise and school accounts with commercial data protection (EDP) are excluded from training.
optOutHow: In consumer Copilot settings, go to Privacy > Model Training and toggle off 'Model Training for Text/Voice'.
retentionPeriod: Consumer conversations are kept in account history for 18 months. If history is disabled, Microsoft retains data for 30 days for abuse monitoring, then deletes it.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: You can delete individual conversations or your entire conversation history at any time through the Copilot UI or the Microsoft Privacy Dashboard.
thirdPartySharing: Shared with service providers and Microsoft affiliates. Query and conversation data is shared with OpenAI under strict confidentiality terms, but OpenAI cannot train on Microsoft user data.
humanReviewConditions: Some conversations are subject to human review for product improvement and digital safety. Conversations flagged as Code of Conduct violations may also be reviewed. Opt-out of human review is not available.
regionalVariation: EEA/UK users have access to GDPR privacy dashboards and enhanced telemetry opt-out options.
childrenDataPolicy: Requires a Microsoft account, which restricts users under 13 (or regional age limit) without explicit parental verification.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Copilot for Microsoft 365 and Copilot with Enterprise Data Protection (EDP) enforce no model training, no human review of chats, and all data is encrypted at rest and in transit.

trainsOnDataNuance: Chats on the consumer Le Chat interface are used for model training by default. Vibe Teams and Enterprise accounts are excluded from training by default. API platform usage is also excluded.
optOutHow: In Le Chat settings, open the Data & Account Controls panel and uncheck the 'Enable data sharing' checkbox. In the Admin Console, disable the toggle under Privacy to stop training on interactions.
retentionPeriod: Conversations are saved to account history indefinitely until deleted by the user. If opted out, inputs and outputs are not collected for training.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: You can delete chats directly in the interface. Deletion is instantaneous in the active system. Backup copies are cleared per standard backup retention cycles.
thirdPartySharing: Shared with cloud infrastructure hosts (such as Scaleway, Azure, and AWS) in compliance with European privacy standards.
humanReviewConditions: Human review is highly restricted, limited to abuse reports, security incidents, or when legally mandated.
regionalVariation: Based in France. GDPR serves as the baseline for all users globally, providing strong default rights.
childrenDataPolicy: The consumer Le Chat service is restricted to users who are 18 years or older.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: The developer API platform does not train on query data by default. Enterprise contracts offer formal GDPR clauses and dedicated deployment options.

trainsOnDataNuance: Consumer queries are used for model training by default. Enterprise Pro accounts are excluded from model training.
optOutHow: In Account Settings, toggle off the 'AI Data Retention' option. Opting out only prevents future queries from being trained on. It does not retroactively remove data already used.
retentionPeriod: Searches and thread history are stored until deleted by the user. Retained for training purposes unless opted out.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: You can delete individual search threads or request full account deletion via account settings or support email. Account deletion aims to remove personal data within 30 days.
thirdPartySharing: Queries may be sent to third-party LLM providers (such as OpenAI and Anthropic) to generate answers, under contracts that prohibit those providers from using Perplexity data for model training.
humanReviewConditions: Conversations may be processed for safety monitoring and performance evaluation, occasionally involving human review of flagged queries.
regionalVariation: Fully compliant with GDPR for EEA users, providing tools to export data, request deletion, and restrict processing.
childrenDataPolicy: Limited to users 13+ (or 16+ in the EEA). Underage accounts are deleted upon discovery.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Perplexity Enterprise Pro features SSO, data encryption, no data training by default, and immediate data deletion upon workspace deletion.

trainsOnDataNuance: Both your direct chats with Grok and your public posts on X are used by default to train Grok models. API platform accounts are excluded from training and covered by separate terms.
optOutHow: On the X platform, go to Settings and Privacy > Privacy and safety > Grok, and toggle off the training option. On the x.ai portal, go to Data Controls and deselect 'Improve the model'.
retentionPeriod: Conversations and posts are retained as long as necessary for model training and service delivery. Private Chat conversations are deleted within 30 days unless legal or safety reasons require longer retention.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Grok chat history can be cleared in the UI. Public posts on X that have already been ingested into training sets cannot be easily purged or recalled. Account deletion also triggers deletion within 30 days.
thirdPartySharing: Shared with X Corp affiliates and service providers under vendor agreements.
humanReviewConditions: Logs may undergo human review for safety filtering, alignment verification, and reinforcement learning purposes.
regionalVariation: GDPR controls are available to EU/UK users. Subject to scrutiny by the Irish Data Protection Commission regarding X post ingestion.
childrenDataPolicy: Age limit is 13+ (aligned with X platform registration terms). Parental consent required for users 13-17.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Developer API platform services have separate terms where prompt inputs are not used for training and are subject to standard enterprise security policies.

trainsOnDataNuance: Free consumer chats on DeepSeek Chat are used to train and optimize models by default. API platform usage is excluded from training.
optOutHow: In consumer settings, turn off the 'Improve the model for everyone' toggle to opt out of the model training program.
retentionPeriod: Retained as long as necessary for business purposes. De-identified or aggregated data may be retained indefinitely.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: You can delete chats from your history or request full account deletion. Deletion requests are processed per local regulations (such as CAC in China and GDPR in Europe).
thirdPartySharing: Shared with affiliates, infrastructure providers, and authorities under Chinese cybersecurity laws and international compliance rules.
humanReviewConditions: Conversations may be reviewed by human annotators for compliance with Chinese safety laws (CAC) and model optimization.
regionalVariation: Domestic Chinese users are subject to CAC regulation. International users have separate privacy policies and data hosted outside mainland China (e.g., Singapore).
childrenDataPolicy: Limited to users 18 years or older. Underage accounts are terminated upon discovery.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: DeepSeek API platform usage does not train on query data by default. It offers temporary data caching (up to 30 days) for abuse detection and complies with standard enterprise security expectations.

trainsOnDataNuance: AWS Bedrock does not use customer data (inputs, outputs, or custom model weights) to train or improve any base models by default. Other AWS AI services may collect data by default but allow account-level opt-out.
optOutHow: Opt-out is the default for Bedrock (no action needed). For other AWS AI services, opt out via AWS Organizations by attaching an AI services opt-out policy.
retentionPeriod: No conversational data is stored by AWS for Bedrock inference. Customer data remains within their selected AWS region VPC.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: You have full ownership and control over your data. It can be deleted instantly through standard AWS console or API commands.
thirdPartySharing: AWS does not share customer prompts or outputs with model providers (such as Anthropic, Meta, or Cohere) or any other third parties.
humanReviewConditions: There is no human review of prompts or responses processed through Amazon Bedrock.
regionalVariation: Compliance is managed at the AWS region level (e.g., EU-West-1 for GDPR alignment, US-East-1 for US compliance).
childrenDataPolicy: AWS is a cloud developer platform restricted to users 18 years or older.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Bedrock is an enterprise developer service with comprehensive B2B compliance (HIPAA, SOC, GDPR), customer-managed encryption keys (KMS), and VPC connectivity.

trainsOnDataNuance: OpenRouter acts as a proxy between users and AI model providers. It does not train on user data. Data is forwarded to the selected model provider.
optOutHow: Not applicable - no training occurs.
retentionPeriod: Minimal - logs only for routing and billing purposes.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: You can delete your account and associated data at any time.
thirdPartySharing: Data is shared with the selected model provider (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) for the purpose of generating responses.
humanReviewConditions: No human review - OpenRouter acts as a transparent proxy only.
regionalVariation: Follows GDPR for EU users. Data handling depends on the underlying model provider's policies.
childrenDataPolicy: Requires users to be at least 13 years old. Users under 18 need permission from a parent or guardian.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Same privacy policy for all users. Acts as a gateway only - data handling determined by selected model provider.

trainsOnDataNuance: Cloudflare AI Gateway acts as a proxy and does not train on user data. Cloudflare's broader privacy policy covers general data handling.
optOutHow: Not applicable - no training occurs.
retentionPeriod: Logs are retained for up to 30 days by default, configurable by customer.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Data can be deleted via API or dashboard at any time.
thirdPartySharing: Data is forwarded to the configured upstream AI provider. Cloudflare does not share data with third parties for training purposes.
humanReviewConditions: No human review of AI Gateway traffic.
regionalVariation: GDPR compliant. Data processing occurs in the customer's selected Cloudflare region.
childrenDataPolicy: Enterprise platform restricted to users 18 years or older.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Same privacy terms for all users. Cloudflare acts as a data processor only.

trainsOnDataNuance: Groq does not train on customer data. Prompts and responses are retained for up to 7 days for abuse monitoring, then permanently deleted.
optOutHow: Not applicable - no training occurs.
retentionPeriod: 7 days for abuse monitoring, then permanently deleted.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Account deletion removes data within 7 days.
thirdPartySharing: No customer data is shared with third parties for training. Data may be shared with service providers for operations.
humanReviewConditions: No human review of customer prompts or responses.
regionalVariation: GDPR compliant for EU users.
childrenDataPolicy: Restricted to users 18 years or older.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Same privacy terms for all users. No training on any customer data.

trainsOnDataNuance: Cerebras does not harvest data from AI interactions. Prompts and outputs are not used for training purposes.
optOutHow: Not applicable - no training occurs.
retentionPeriod: Minimal retention for service operations. Data is not retained for training.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Account and data deletion available upon request.
thirdPartySharing: No customer data is shared for training purposes.
humanReviewConditions: No human review of customer data.
regionalVariation: US-based company with standard privacy protections.
childrenDataPolicy: Requires users to be at least 13 years old. Users under 18 need permission from a parent or guardian.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Same privacy terms for all users.

trainsOnDataNuance: Fireworks AI does not use customer data to train models without explicit opt-in. Zero Data Retention is available for open models by default.
optOutHow: Not applicable - training requires explicit opt-in.
retentionPeriod: Zero Data Retention by default for open models. Enterprise plans offer custom retention.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Data deletion available via API or support request.
thirdPartySharing: No customer data is shared for training purposes.
humanReviewConditions: No human review of customer prompts or outputs.
regionalVariation: US-based company with GDPR compliance for EU users.
childrenDataPolicy: Requires users to be at least 13 years old. Users under 18 need permission from a parent or guardian.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Enterprise accounts offer custom data retention and compliance options.

trainsOnDataNuance: Together AI does not use customer data to train models without explicit opt-in. Zero Data Retention for open models by default.
optOutHow: Not applicable - training requires explicit opt-in.
retentionPeriod: Zero by default for open models. Enterprise plans offer custom retention.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Data deletion available via API or support request.
thirdPartySharing: No customer data is shared for training purposes.
humanReviewConditions: No human review of customer prompts or outputs.
regionalVariation: US-based company with GDPR compliance for EU users.
childrenDataPolicy: Requires users to be at least 13 years old. Users under 18 need permission from a parent or guardian.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Enterprise accounts offer custom data retention and compliance options.

trainsOnDataNuance: NVIDIA does not use customer data to train AI models. NIM runs inference only. NVIDIA's broader privacy policy covers general data collection for services.
optOutHow: Not applicable - no training on customer AI data.
retentionPeriod: Standard business data retention. No AI-specific data retention for training.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Data deletion available via NVIDIA account privacy settings.
thirdPartySharing: Shared with service providers for operations. Not used for AI training.
humanReviewConditions: No human review of customer AI inference data.
regionalVariation: GDPR compliant. Data processing occurs in customer's selected region.
childrenDataPolicy: Enterprise platform restricted to users 18 years or older.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Same privacy terms for all users. No training on customer data.

trainsOnDataNuance: Sarvam AI does not use customer content to train AI models unless explicit opt-in is provided.
optOutHow: Not applicable - training requires explicit opt-in.
retentionPeriod: Data retained as necessary for service operations.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Account deletion and data removal available upon request.
thirdPartySharing: No customer data is shared for training purposes.
humanReviewConditions: No human review of customer data.
regionalVariation: India-based company with standard privacy protections.
childrenDataPolicy: Restricted to users 18 years or older.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Same privacy terms for all users.

trainsOnDataNuance: Z.ai does not use customer data for AI model training without explicit consent.
optOutHow: Not applicable - training requires explicit opt-in.
retentionPeriod: Data retained as necessary for service operations.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Data deletion available upon request.
thirdPartySharing: No customer data is shared for training purposes.
humanReviewConditions: No human review of customer data.
regionalVariation: Standard privacy protections apply.
childrenDataPolicy: Restricted to users 18 years or older.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Same privacy terms for all users.

trainsOnDataNuance: Kong AI Gateway is an API gateway that processes requests but does not train on customer data. Acts as a processor only.
optOutHow: Not applicable - no training occurs.
retentionPeriod: Logs retained for operational purposes. No AI-specific data retention.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Data deletion available via configuration.
thirdPartySharing: Data is forwarded to configured upstream services only.
humanReviewConditions: No human review of gateway traffic.
regionalVariation: GDPR compliant for EU users.
childrenDataPolicy: Enterprise platform restricted to users 18 years or older.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Same privacy terms for all users. Acts as a processor only.

trainsOnDataNuance: Portkey acts as an AI gateway and does not train on customer data. Data is forwarded to the selected model provider.
optOutHow: Not applicable - no training occurs.
retentionPeriod: Minimal logging for operations. No AI-specific data retention.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Data deletion available via API.
thirdPartySharing: Data is forwarded to the configured upstream AI provider.
humanReviewConditions: No human review of gateway traffic.
regionalVariation: Standard privacy protections apply.
childrenDataPolicy: Restricted to users 18 years or older.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Same privacy terms for all users.

trainsOnDataNuance: LiteLLM is an open-source proxy. No data training occurs - it simply routes requests to model providers.
optOutHow: Not applicable - no training occurs (open-source software).
retentionPeriod: No data retention by default. Self-hosted solution.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: N/A - self-hosted, user controls all data.
thirdPartySharing: No data sharing - requests forwarded to configured model providers.
humanReviewConditions: No human review - open-source proxy software.
regionalVariation: N/A - self-hosted solution.
childrenDataPolicy: N/A - developer tool.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Same privacy terms for all users - self-hosted open-source software.

trainsOnDataNuance: Haystack is an open-source framework for building AI applications. No data training occurs - it's a development tool.
optOutHow: Not applicable - no training occurs (open-source software).
retentionPeriod: No data retention - self-hosted open-source framework.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: N/A - self-hosted, user controls all data.
thirdPartySharing: No data sharing - framework runs locally.
humanReviewConditions: No human review - open-source framework.
regionalVariation: N/A - self-hosted solution.
childrenDataPolicy: N/A - developer tool.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Same privacy terms for all users - self-hosted open-source software.

trainsOnDataNuance: Enterprise accounts do not train on customer data by default. Trial and Research accounts may use de-identified data for R&D. Cohere does not intentionally collect personal information for training.
optOutHow: Not applicable for enterprise - no training by default. Trial users can upgrade to enterprise for full data protection.
retentionPeriod: Enterprise: 30 days for inputs/outputs. Trial/Research: Data used for R&D (de-identified).
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Account deletion and data removal available. Enterprise accounts have formal DPA options.
thirdPartySharing: Shared with service providers under strict agreements. Not used for third-party training.
humanReviewConditions: No human review of enterprise customer data.
regionalVariation: GDPR compliant. Canadian company with EU presence. SOC 2 certified.
childrenDataPolicy: Not intended for use by minors. Restricted to users 18 years or older.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Enterprise accounts have formal data protection agreements, no training, and 30-day retention.

trainsOnDataNuance: Hugging Face does not sell or rent personal information. Models are opt-in for training. Inference API does not train on customer data by default.
optOutHow: Not applicable - no training on customer data by default.
retentionPeriod: Data retained as long as necessary to deliver services and comply with legal requirements.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Account deletion and data removal available via privacy@huggingface.co.
thirdPartySharing: Shared with service providers (AWS, GCP, etc.) under strict data privacy agreements.
humanReviewConditions: No human review of customer inference data.
regionalVariation: French company with EU presence. GDPR compliant. CNIL registered.
childrenDataPolicy: Not directed to children under 16 (or 13 with parental consent where local law permits). Services intended for developers and researchers.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Enterprise Hub accounts offer enhanced privacy controls and SOC 2 compliance.

trainsOnDataNuance: Replicate does not sell or share personal information. Acts as a platform for running models - does not train on customer data by default.
optOutHow: Not applicable - no training on customer data.
retentionPeriod: Retained as long as necessary to provide services. Abuse detection and legal compliance.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Account deletion and data removal available via privacy@replicate.com.
thirdPartySharing: Shared with service providers for operations. Not sold to advertisers.
humanReviewConditions: No human review of customer model inputs/outputs.
regionalVariation: US-based company (San Francisco). Standard privacy protections.
childrenDataPolicy: Not intended for users under 16. Services designed for business professionals.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Same privacy terms for all users.

trainsOnDataNuance: SambaNova does not sell personal information. Uses data for service operations and improvement. No explicit AI training on customer data.
optOutHow: Opt-out available for marketing emails via opt-out@sambanova.ai.
retentionPeriod: Retained during contractual relationship plus limitation period. Marketing: 3 years. Job applicants: 2 years.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Deletion available via privacy@sambanova.ai. Retention per policy terms.
thirdPartySharing: Shared with affiliates and service providers under strict agreements. Not sold.
humanReviewConditions: No human review of customer AI data.
regionalVariation: US-based company (San Jose, CA). GDPR compliant for EU users.
childrenDataPolicy: Does not knowingly collect data from children under 16.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Same privacy terms for all users.

trainsOnDataNuance: Nous Research is a research organization focused on open-source models. Does not operate a commercial inference service that trains on user data.
optOutHow: Not applicable - no commercial training service.
retentionPeriod: N/A - research organization, no user data collection.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: N/A - minimal data collection.
thirdPartySharing: N/A - open-source research organization.
humanReviewConditions: N/A - research organization.
regionalVariation: N/A - open-source research.
childrenDataPolicy: N/A - research organization.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: N/A - research organization.

trainsOnDataNuance: Prompts and inputs are processed in real time and not saved on the servers for model training.
optOutHow: Not applicable because the platform does not train on user prompts.
retentionPeriod: IP addresses are kept for 3 years. Prompts and inputs are processed in real time and not stored.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Accounts and associated technical logs can be deleted upon request.
thirdPartySharing: Disclosed to server deployment, database storage, SMS providers, and affiliates.
humanReviewConditions: No human review of chat content is mentioned in the policy.
regionalVariation: Global platform subject to standard regional privacy regulations including GDPR and US state laws.
childrenDataPolicy: Not intended for minors under 18 years old.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Explains processor role for commercial customers who serve as data controllers for their end users.

trainsOnDataNuance: Voice and text inputs are processed in real time and not saved on the servers.
optOutHow: Not applicable because inputs are not saved or trained on.
retentionPeriod: Retained for the shortest necessary period to provide services.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Users can request deletion of real-name authentication details, which stops corresponding services.
thirdPartySharing: Shared with transaction payment institutions and technical system service providers.
humanReviewConditions: No human review of chat content is mentioned in the policy.
regionalVariation: Based in China and subject to Chinese data protection regulations.
childrenDataPolicy: Children under 14 years old cannot create accounts without guardian consent.

trainsOnDataNuance: Ollama runs locally on user hardware. No cloud service, no data collection, no training on user data.

thirdPartySharing: No data sharing - fully local.

regionalVariation: N/A - local execution.
childrenDataPolicy: N/A - local software.

humanReviewConditions: No human review of customer data.
regionalVariation: US-based company. Standard privacy protections.
childrenDataPolicy: Enterprise platform restricted to users 18 years or older.

trainsOnDataNuance: Paid models use zero data retention; free tier models may log and retain data to improve the models.
optOutHow: Workspace admins can disable specific models that collect data.
retentionPeriod: Zero data retention for paid models; up to 30 days for upstream providers (OpenAI/Anthropic).
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Account data and logs can be deleted upon request or by configuring retention rules.
thirdPartySharing: Data is forwarded to the configured upstream AI providers (OpenAI, Anthropic).
humanReviewConditions: No human review of chats on paid models.
regionalVariation: Standard regional compliance as an API gateway service.
childrenDataPolicy: Enterprise platform restricted to users 18 years or older.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Paid models use zero data retention; free tier models may retain data to improve models.

trainsOnDataNuance: Free and Pro accounts train on your prompts and code. Business and Enterprise tiers turn off training by default.
optOutHow: Toggle Privacy Mode in Cursor settings (General tab) or in your online account settings.
retentionPeriod: Cursor retains data when Privacy Mode is off. Turning it on ensures zero data retention for upstream models, and Cursor avoids storing prompts.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: You can request account deletion to remove personal data, though the company keeps anonymous telemetry.
thirdPartySharing: Sends data to OpenAI and Anthropic under zero data retention terms when you use Privacy Mode.
humanReviewConditions: Staff review conversations only if automated filters trigger safety or abuse alerts.
regionalVariation: GDPR protections apply for EU and UK users, including data deletion and processing objection.
childrenDataPolicy: Requires users to be at least 13 years old.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Enterprise plans enable Privacy Mode by default, ensuring zero data retention and no model training.

trainsOnDataNuance: Stores and uses your chat history and code snippets for AI training unless you turn on Privacy Mode.
optOutHow: Turn on Privacy Mode in the IDE settings. This requires you to stay logged in.
retentionPeriod: Privacy Mode stops training on your data. Otherwise, the app stores chat history and telemetry for optimization.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: Account deletion removes personal files and info. Telemetry data may persist.
thirdPartySharing: Shares telemetry and logs with ByteDance and affiliates. The app deletes plaintext codebase files after computing embeddings.
humanReviewConditions: Staff review conversations and telemetry to check for policy violations and security issues.
regionalVariation: Operated by TikTok USDS Joint Venture LLC in the US, with specific regional telemetry policies.
childrenDataPolicy: The IDE is not for children under 13.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: All accounts use the same privacy policy. You can exclude specific files using a .aiignore file.

trainsOnDataNuance: Does not train models on private user code. Zero Data Retention is active by default.
optOutHow: Zero Data Retention is active by default. You can adjust telemetry settings in your web profile or extension options.
retentionPeriod: The servers do not store plaintext code. The app keeps telemetry data to monitor performance.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: You can delete your account and associated metadata by contacting support.
thirdPartySharing: Uses telemetry internally. Under Zero Data Retention, the app processes code in transit and does not share it for model training.
humanReviewConditions: No human reviews private code or chat history. Telemetry is reviewed to monitor performance.
regionalVariation: EU users have standard GDPR rights, including data access and deletion.
childrenDataPolicy: Intended for users 13 years of age or older.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Teams and Enterprise plans offer dedicated deployment options and guaranteed Zero Data Retention. Cognition acquired the brand in 2025.

trainsOnDataNuance: Does not use customer data to train models unless you explicitly opt in.
optOutHow: Not applicable because training is off by default. You can email security@cognition.ai for specific data requests.
retentionPeriod: Retains customer data for the duration of the business relationship. The app keeps feedback and interaction logs as long as needed.
dataDeletedOnRequestTimeframe: You can request account and data deletion by contacting support or emailing the privacy team.
thirdPartySharing: Shares data with service providers under data processing agreements. Telemetry sent to external integrations depends on their policies.
humanReviewConditions: Staff review telemetry, interaction logs, and flagged agent runs to fix bugs and monitor security.
regionalVariation: Complies with GDPR; EU users can contact privacy@cognition.ai for data requests.
childrenDataPolicy: Designed for users 18 years or older.
enterpriseVsConsumerSummary: Enterprise accounts use custom agreements that can specify private cloud deployments and custom data retention times.
